title: Dishonour and Return of Cheques
body_text:
Dishonour and Return of Cheques
1	Introduction to Dishonour and Return of Cheques
1.1	Overview of Dishonour and Return of Cheques
Your financial institution can dishonour a cheque drawn on a memberГўВҖВҷs account for several reasons (e.g., insufficient funds). When your financial institution dishonours a cheque, it is returned to the Negotiating Financial Institution through the clearing system following rules and time frames provided by Payments Canada. Your financial institution can lose recourse if these rules and time frames arenГўВҖВҷt followed.
Cheques are typically returned electronically via the Online Return System. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures. This Part provides procedures on manual cheque returns for the circumstances when a cheque canГўВҖВҷt be returned electronically. It also identifies the business rules associated with dishonouring and returning cheques.
Related Payment Solutions Manual Parts
Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part
Deposit Processing Part
File Exchange and Report Distribution Part
Online Return System (ORS) Part
AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part
1.2	Return Reason Codes and Time Frames
CPA Rule A4, Returned and Redirected Items, outlines the procedures, time frames, and responsibilities related to returning a dishonoured cheque. In most cases, a cheque must be returned to the Negotiating Financial Institution no later than the business day following receipt of the cheque by your financial institution. However, longer time frames are allowed when returning cheques for certain reasons.
For a full list of cheque return reason codes and the corresponding time frames for return, refer to the Online Return System (ORS) Part, Appendix #A-5, Cheque Return Reason Codes.
Note:	The CPA time frames can be extended if items are delayed due to
force majeure
. Force majeure is an act that occurs beyond your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs control (i.e., natural disaster, fire, riot, etc.), which prevents it from returning items within normal time frames. Payments Canada must authorize all return time frame extensions due to force majeure. Refer to CPA Rule J7, Force Majeure Situations.
Recourse rights can be lost if a cheque isnГўВҖВҷt returned per the CPA Rules, including CPA time frames.
1.3	Contacts and Websites
Resources/Services
Contact Information
Contact the Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction to verify the validity of a BC Employment and Assistance Cheque. Call this number only if you have a specific reason to be concerned about the validity of a cheque.

778-405-4306
or
